data.body:
OVERLAY CATALOG — accessibility (a11y)  [PROJECT-DEPENDENT]
- Source: WCAG 2.2 AA (W3C license; authoritative criteria) + eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y (MIT; directly reusable rule set) + axe-core rule TAXONOMY (MPL — reuse concepts only, do NOT vendor source) + WAI-ARIA APG + ACT Rules (W3C). No turnkey LLM rubric (Microsoft a11y-llm-eval / CodeA11y = prompt-design refs only); author from the linter taxonomy validated vs WCAG 2.2 AA.
- Dimensions: D1 text alternatives (alt); D2 forms/labels; D3 color/contrast (>=4.5:1; verify-rendered flag); D4 keyboard/focus (+ WCAG 2.2 target-size>=24px, dragging-alternative, focus-not-obscured); D5 ARIA correctness; D6 name/role/value (vs APG patterns); D7 semantic HTML; D8 headings/landmarks; D9 lang/document; D10 media/motion. Impact tiers: critical/serious/moderate/minor.
- Triggers: *.jsx/tsx/vue/svelte/astro/html + templates (erb/hbs/blade/twig/liquid/razor/cshtml) + css/scss + {components,ui,views,pages,layouts}/ dirs + *.stories.*; content signals (JSX, role=/aria-*, onClick, dangerouslySetInnerHTML, .focus()).
- Base-escalation: interactive element / ARIA / tabindex / focus mgmt / contrast literal / custom widget in ANY file.
- RELEVANCE FLAG: project-dependent — HIGH for frontend/UI, LOW-TO-ZERO for backend/CLI/library/infra (incl. rebar itself). Gate on UI globs + a repo-level "frontend present?" heuristic; keep dormant on non-UI repos to avoid noise.
- CEILING FLAG: rendered contrast, focus visibility, screen-reader announcement order, decorative-vs-meaningful alt are NOT statically verifiable -> emit as "manual verification required" (pass-2 "insufficient" verdict), never pass/fail.
- LICENSE: use jsx-a11y (MIT) + W3C criteria; do NOT vendor axe-core (MPL) / pa11y HTMLCS (LGPL) source; WAVE proprietary -> avoid.
